en.title: Massive data: in-depth summary
en.prompt: Based on the content from articles '@1' to '@3', please analyze and identify any errors or inconsistencies that need to be corrected or supplemented. Make sure your analysis is consistent with the original content of the articles.
en.remark: For an in-depth summary, it is recommended to ask two rounds of questions. During the second inquiry, have it refer back to the original text to check for errors or omissions. Excerpted from an article by Esor Huang.

related.title: Writing assistant
related.remark: For optimising the grammar, clarity and conciseness of text and improving readability.

related.title: Nature Style Editing
related.remark: Polish according to the style of Nature, or provide a writing style to emulate. Contributed by @Pfyuan77.

metaDescription: Analyzing text? Identify errors and inconsistencies across multiple articles. Ensure logical flow and factual accuracy. Try it now.

faq.a: The first round may include AI fabrication or omissions. The second round requires "check for errors and omissions against the original," forcing the AI back to the source. Single summaries drift easily; the double-pass mechanism is a practical trick against AI hallucination.

faq.q: Does the AI actually find errors on second check?
faq.a: It finds obvious errors (dates, numbers), but deeper drift (tone, implied stance) is still hard to catch. For more rigor, a third pass asking the AI to "list all key points omitted from the original" surfaces more overlooked details than two passes.
